result:
Done. The spec's three missing operations are implemented in `CsvRow.cs` and the project builds cleanly with no warnings.

- **`EscapeField`** (CsvRow.cs:16) returns the field verbatim unless `NeedsQuoting` holds, in which case it doubles internal quotes and wraps the result in double-quotes.
- **`JoinRow`** (CsvRow.cs:25) escapes each field and joins with commas; the empty list yields the empty string via `string.Join`.
- **`SplitRow`** (CsvRow.cs:30) is a single left-to-right scan. A field starting with `"` is consumed until the closing un-doubled quote, with `""` contributing one literal quote and commas/CR/LF inside treated as content; other fields run to the next comma or end of line. The loop structure guarantees the empty line parses to one empty field and a trailing comma produces a final empty field, so `SplitRow(JoinRow(fields))` round-trips every non-empty list.
